answer: Patellar apprehension; Patellar Subluxation or Patellar Retinaculum Sprain
FABER; Hip pathology, sacroiliac joint pathology, iliopsoas tightness
Ober; Tight IT band or tight TFL
Thomas; Rectus femoris tightness or iliopsoas tightness
AC compression; Acromioclavicular separation or ligament sprain and/or coracoclavicular sprain
AC distraction; Compromise of acromioclavicular ligament
Empty can; Weakness or tear of supraspinatus
Hawkins kennedy; Shoulder impingement of supraspinatus or long head of biceps brachii
Neer; Shoulder impingement of supraspinatus and long head of biceps tendon
Piano key; Instability of AC joint
Yergason; Bicipital tendinitis or tear or laxity of transverse humeral ligament
Push off; Subscapularis rupture or dysfunction
Hornblower; Strength and integrity of teres minor
Slump; Identifies neural tension, dural involvement, or sciatic nerve compression
Allen; Partial or complete occlusion of the radial or ulnar arteries
Finkelstein; Tenosynovitis of the pollicis longus and extensor pollicis brevis tendons
Phalen; Carpal tunnel syndrome or median nerve compression
Tinel; Compression of median nerve in the carpal tunnel or carpal tunnel syndrome
